home.heroDescription: Passionate about web and software development and solving complex problems. Currently in my third year at Polytechnique Montréal.
home.crossPlayDescription: Architecture synchronizing a Desktop client and a Mobile app for a unified real-time Cross-Play experience.
home.rpgDescription: Real-time multiplayer full-stack application with distributed architecture and automated deployment.
home.robotDescription: Autonomous multi-robot system with infrared communication, mapping, and microcontroller navigation.
home.academicDescription: Coming from a background in Pure and Applied Sciences, I built a strong mathematical foundation that I now apply to software architecture.
home.proSubtitle: Former Line Cook at La Cage Place Bell
home.proDescription: Working in one of Laval's busiest restaurants taught me strong discipline. I learned how to handle intense peak times, prioritize tasks in real time, and communicate effectively with a team to deliver quality under pressure.

projectRpg.title: Multiplayer Tactical RPG
projectRpg.description: Design of a full-stack multiplayer game application (Angular/Node.js), integrating real-time synchronization (WebSockets) and a continuous deployment pipeline (CI/CD).
projectRpg.challengeDescription: Build a robust full-stack application able to handle multiplayer matches. The challenge was orchestrating a complete distributed architecture, including real-time WebSocket synchronization, CI/CD deployment to AWS and GitLab Pages, and strong quality assurance with unit tests and code reviews.
projectRpg.contributionDescription: Within a Scrum team, I actively contributed to the entire development lifecycle, without limiting myself to one technology:
projectRpg.role1: Cross-Functional Development: Implemented complete features from Angular UI to Node.js server logic and database layers.
projectRpg.role2: Quality Assurance: Wrote unit tests (Jest/Mocha/Chai) and validated code through rigorous merge request reviews.
projectRpg.role3: DevOps & Architecture: Configured CI/CD pipeline and contributed to architecture decisions (UML modeling, REST API).
projectRpg.algorithmCaption: Demo: The weighted BFS algorithm computes reachable movement area. Notice how range is reduced on water (high cost), blocked by walls, and extended on ice.
projectRpg.resultDescription: Application deployed in production on AWS through an automated CI/CD pipeline. The system handles multiplayer concurrency reliably and preserves full integrity of created and updated game histories.

projectRobot.title: Autonomous Navigation Robot
projectRobot.description: Design of an autonomous navigation system capable of mapping and crossing an obstacle course in real time.
projectRobot.challengeDescription: The project required coordinating two distinct robots. The first explored and mapped obstacles with a line sensor, then transmitted data through infrared to the second robot in a closed zone, which had to orient itself and exit based on information received from the distance sensor.
projectRobot.responsibilityDescription: In a 4-person team, I had full responsibility for the receiver robot while ensuring overall system integration:
projectRobot.role1: Software Architecture (C): Designed a complex state machine to manage phases (Wait, Receive, Center, Exit) deterministically.
projectRobot.role2: Control & Sensors: Implemented trajectory correction algorithms (control loop) to ensure precise movement despite mechanical imperfections.
projectRobot.role3: Technical Collaboration: Supported development of the second robot (line following) to ensure the full chain worked on demo day.
projectRobot.img2Caption: Motherboard used for each robot (architecture provided by INF1900)
projectRobot.techSub1: Bare-metal C/C++ programming, register manipulation & interrupts.
projectRobot.techSub3: Analog/digital reading, auto calibration & PID algorithm.
projectRobot.techDesc4: RX: Panasonic PNA4602 | TX: Lite-On LTE 4208
projectRobot.actionCaption: Demo: Robot 2 detects and maps the line, then continues navigation autonomously.
projectRobot.resultDescription: The robot demonstrated full autonomy without human intervention, executing the exit scenario without line deviation and within time limits, validating the robustness of the embedded logic.

rpgEvolution.title: Distributed & Mobile Cross-Play Ecosystem
rpgEvolution.description: Transformation of a monolithic web architecture into a distributed multi-client system. Real-time synchronization between a Mobile app (thin client) and an existing Desktop client (thick client), with technical debt management.
rpgEvolution.challengeDescription: Transform the web platform into a market-ready multi-platform product. Beyond distributed architecture, the major challenge is a complete UI/UX redesign to adapt desktop controls to mobile format, while integrating new features under strict SRS requirements and maintainability constraints.
rpgEvolution.role1: Architecture & Cloud: AWS distributed solution synchronizing heterogeneous clients via Socket.io.
rpgEvolution.role2: Mobile Development: Transposed desktop UX into an intuitive touch interface (Dart) integrating native sensors.
rpgEvolution.role3: Engineering: Led requirements (SRS) and simulated budget management (~1000h).
rpgEvolution.role4: Integration: Implemented new features in legacy codebase while ensuring non-regression.
rpgEvolution.statusNote: Final delivery planned for May 2026. Source code will be available afterward.
